description: LLM hallucination facts with deliberately INCOHERENT distractors. Distractors are designed to be grammatically awkward or semantically contradictory to the context.

facts.context: LLM hallucination refers to model outputs that are:
facts.truth: fluent but factually incorrect or fabricated
distractors: the training data was purple yesterday
mathematical proofs about cooking recipes
underwater basket weaving certification exams

facts.context: Closed-book QA hallucination rates are problematic because:
facts.truth: models confabulate plausible but false details
distractors: the color green tastes like Thursday mornings
triangles have seventeen perpendicular sides
photosynthesis requires volcanic eruptions

facts.context: RAG does not eliminate hallucination because:
facts.truth: models may ignore or hallucinate beyond context
distractors: refrigerators dream about electric sheep nightly
the square root of furniture equals happiness
temporal paradoxes dissolve into marmalade

facts.truth: fake papers, authors, and DOIs are fabricated
distractors: the moon is made of crystallized opinions
gravity works sideways on alternate Tuesdays
electrons prefer jazz music to classical

facts.context: LLM confidence calibration is problematic because:
facts.truth: high confidence even when factually wrong
distractors: the number seven smells like wet concrete
mitochondria vote in local elections weekly
algebra was invented by confused dolphins

facts.context: Teaching LLMs to say 'I don't know' is difficult because:
facts.truth: training rewards plausible over honest answers
distractors: the concept of nothing weighs exactly pi grams
existential dread causes hardware failures daily
philosophy students photosynthesize at night

facts.truth: models blend attributes of similar entities
distractors: the periodic table was written by angry clouds
synonyms taste better when refrigerated properly
historical figures communicate through mushrooms

facts.context: Temporal hallucination in LLMs refers to:
facts.truth: mixing up dates and temporal ordering errors
distractors: the future smells like burnt orange peels
clocks measure the density of regret accurately
yesterday happens after next Thursday sometimes

facts.truth: reducing variance but not systematic errors
distractors: teaching fish to appreciate modern architecture
converting emotional states into prime numbers
making clouds more aerodynamic during rainfall

facts.context: Attribution in LLMs is challenging because:
distractors: footnotes are allergic to the letter Q
bibliographies migrate south for the winter
citations reproduce asexually in dark spaces

facts.truth: long-tail entities and rare training facts
distractors: the speed of light varies with humidity levels
common knowledge ferments into cheese slowly
memories are stored in parallel universes only

facts.context: Automated hallucination detection is difficult because:
facts.truth: requires external verification that may be incomplete
distractors: detection algorithms are afraid of the dark
verification requires permission from ghosts
factual errors taste like strawberry jam
